prompt:
Our order-intake service started 500ing last night on a slice of checkouts and I traced it into the shipping module. Symptom: any order whose timestamp arrives with a UTC offset blows up inside `CutoffCalculator.makes_cutoff` with:

    TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes

Plain wall-clock timestamps (what the old POS sends) work fine, which is why this sailed through review. The new storefront stamps orders in UTC and mobile stamps them in whatever zone the phone is in, and the ones near midnight UTC also come out with the wrong ship day when people 'fix' it locally by lopping the offset off — an order at 03:30Z on the 7th is still the evening of the 6th at the warehouse and has missed the 6th's pickup. We take orders across the DST changes too, and fold-annotated wall times during the November repeat hour have to keep meaning what they say.

Repro: `python3 test_cutoff.py` — the aware-input checks explode with the TypeError, and the rest document the decisions we need: at-or-before the cutoff wins, the warehouse zone governs the calendar day, and deadlines come back zone-aware. Fix cutoff.py so the whole thing runs on one coherent time model. Do NOT solve it by stripping timezone info off inputs, and don't touch the tests.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0 on the buggy fixture; test_cutoff.py exits 1 with TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes raised from makes_cutoff on the first offset-aware order (all naive-input checks pass first)
